message:
feat(repair): add self-healing demo module with adoption-wedge example and tests

Implements the adoption wedge for the FrankenFS self-healing story (bd-6tme.1):
a single-command demo that creates a small image, injects corruption, repairs
using RaptorQ symbols, and verifies all checksums match — proving zero data loss.

Changes:
- Add `demo` module to ffs-repair crate with corruption injection and repair logic
- Add `self_heal_demo` example: single-command entry point (`cargo run --example self_heal_demo`)
- Add integration test verifying demo exits cleanly and produces correct output
- Update README with one-liner command for the adoption wedge
- Update beads issue tracker: close bd-6tme.1 (adoption wedge task completed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-6st","title":"Harness: Establish parity accounting protocol (FEATURE_PARITY.md <-> ParityReport)","description":"Goal: ensure we never lose track of what is implemented.

 {"id":"bd-6tme","title":"EPIC: Killer Demo — Self-Healing Durability","description":"# EPIC: Killer Demo — Self-Healing Durability

## PURPOSE
Create a compelling, CI-executable demonstration of FrankenFS self-healing: flip random bits in blocks, show automatic repair using RaptorQ symbols, and output a human-readable repair ledger explaining exactly what was reconstructed. This is the "show, don't tell" proof of FrankenFS's core innovation.

## DEMO SCRIPT

### Setup (automated)
1. Create 128MB ext4 image with FrankenFS (RaptorQ enabled, 5% overhead)
2. Write 100 files with known content (photos, text, binaries)
3. Record BLAKE3 checksums for all files
4. Print: "Filesystem created: 100 files, all checksums verified"

### Corruption (visible to audience)
5. Print: "Injecting corruption: flipping random bits in 3% of blocks..."
6. Corrupt 3% of data blocks with random bit flips
7. Print block IDs and bit positions of corrupted blocks
8. Attempt to read files — show which files have corrupted bytes

### Repair (the magic moment)
9. Print: "Triggering self-healing scrub..."
10. Run scrub daemon (or explicit repair command)
11. Show real-time progress: "Scanning block group 1/32... corruption detected at block 1042... repairing with 4 symbols... OK"
12. Print: "Repair complete in 2.3 seconds"

### Verification (proof)
13. Read all 100 files, verify BLAKE3 checksums
14. Print: "All 100 files verified — zero data loss"
15. Print repair ledger: table of every repair action with timing, blocks, symbols used

### Output Format
```
=== FrankenFS Self-Healing Demo ===
Created: 100 files (128MB image, 5% RaptorQ overhead)
Corrupted: 47 blocks across 8 block groups (3% corruption rate)

Repair Results:
  Block Group  Corrupted  Repaired  Symbols Used  Time
  1            3          3         4             12ms
  4            8          8         12            34ms
  7            6          6         8             28ms
  ...
  Total        47         47        72            180ms

Verification: 100/100 files checksums match — ZERO DATA LOSS
Evidence ledger: 47 CorruptionDetected + 47 RepairSucceeded records
```

## REQUIREMENTS
- Must run without root (FUSE user mount)
- Must complete in < 30 seconds
- Must be deterministic (seeded corruption)
- Must be a single command: `cargo run --bin ffs-demo -- self-healing`
- Must work in CI (no GUI, no interactive input)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Demo runs successfully with single command
- [ ] All corrupted blocks repaired
- [ ] Repair ledger is human-readable and correct
- [ ] Demo completes in < 30 seconds
- [ ] Runs in CI without root
- [ ] Output suitable for README/blog post

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. Demo runs with single command in < 30s
3. All corrupted blocks repaired (zero data loss)
4. Output suitable for README/blog post
5. CI-executable without root or FUSE","notes":"DEPENDENCY NOTE: This demo is the capstone of the self-healing story. It depends on bd-15c.11 (5% corruption E2E test) which depends on the full scrub daemon pipeline. Do not attempt this until bd-15c.11 passes. The demo script should reuse the same test infrastructure but with human-readable output formatting. Consider adding a --verbose flag for technical audiences and a --summary flag for non-technical audiences.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:29:44.707195327Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:26.179756128Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","selfhealing","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6tme","depends_on_id":"bd-15c.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:31.502093882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":97,"issue_id":"bd-6tme","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3. Feature parity gate green'","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:41Z"},{"id":167,"issue_id":"bd-6tme","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

EV score: 25 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: decode_budget_ms=200, io_budget_mb=64, max_repair_attempts=3
Fallback trigger: read-only degrade + evidence ledger + manual repair workflow

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:26Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-6tme.1","title":"Adoption wedge: single-command corruption recovery demo for README","description":"# Adoption wedge: single-command corruption recovery demo for README

## GOAL
Create the simplest possible adoption wedge: a single command that demonstrates FrankenFS self-healing in under 10 seconds, suitable for copy-paste into a README or blog post.

## BACKGROUND
From alien_cs_graveyard.md methodology: every graveyard entry must have an adoption wedge -- the smallest artifact that proves the idea works and makes skeptics try it. bd-6tme (Killer Demo) is the full 30-second version. This bead is the MINIMAL wedge: the thing someone runs in 5 seconds to see the value proposition.

## DELIVERABLES
1. `cargo run --example self_heal_demo` -- creates 8MB image, writes 10 files, corrupts 2%, repairs, verifies
2. Output: 6 lines of text showing create -> corrupt -> repair -> verify cycle
3. Zero dependencies beyond the workspace (no FUSE mount needed -- operates on raw image)
4. Runs in CI, deterministic, < 5 seconds

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: demo script exits 0 and output matches expected pattern
2. Integration: verify checksums correct after repair

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Demo start (info): image_size, file_count, corruption_pct
- Corruption injected (info): blocks_corrupted, seed
- Repair complete (info): blocks_repaired, duration_ms
- Verification (info): files_verified, all_ok

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Single command, zero config, < 5 seconds
2. Output is copy-paste-ready for README
3. Deterministic (seeded)
4.  {"id":"bd-6tme.2","title":"Adoption wedge: deterministic MVCC snapshot isolation demo","description":"# Adoption wedge: deterministic MVCC snapshot isolation demo

## GOAL
Single-command demo showing MVCC snapshot isolation on a filesystem: two concurrent readers see consistent snapshots while a writer modifies files. The adoption wedge for the MVCC story.

## DELIVERABLES
1. `cargo run --example mvcc_demo` -- creates image, starts 2 read transactions + 1 write transaction, shows isolation
2. Output shows: reader A sees version 1, writer commits version 2, reader A still sees version 1, reader B (started after commit) sees version 2
3. No FUSE mount needed -- operates on raw image via API
4. Deterministic, < 3 seconds

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: demo exits 0 with expected output pattern
2. Integration: snapshot isolation invariant verified

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Transaction start (info): txn_id, snapshot_seq
- Read (debug): txn_id, block, version_seen
- Write commit (info): txn_id, commit_seq, blocks_written
- Isolation check (info): reader_txn, expected_version, actual_version, isolated=true/false

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Single command, < 3 seconds
2. Demonstrates snapshot isolation visually
3. Deterministic
4. CI-executable","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:10.338274339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:18.886575988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","mvcc","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6tme.2","depends_on_id":"bd-6tme","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:10.338274339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":122,"issue_id":"bd-6tme.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-6tme.3","title":"E2E test: self-healing demo runs successfully and produces correct output","description":"# E2E test: self-healing demo runs successfully and produces correct output

## GOAL
E2E test for the Killer Demo epic (bd-6tme). Runs the demo script end-to-end and verifies the output format, repair correctness, and timing.

## TEST SCENARIO
1. Run `cargo run --bin ffs-demo -- self-healing` (or equivalent)
2. Verify exit code 0
3. Parse output: verify corruption count, repair count, verification pass
4. Verify: corrupted blocks == repaired blocks (zero data loss)
5. Verify: evidence ledger present and parseable
6. Verify: total time < 30 seconds

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Demo runs without error
2. All corrupted blocks repaired
3. Output matches expected format
4. Timing within bounds
5.  {"id":"bd-7tn4","title":"PoR for cryptographic durability audit (Graveyard Entry 11.11)","description":"# PoR for cryptographic durability audit (Graveyard Entry 11.11)

## GOAL
Implement Proofs of Retrievability (PoR) for cryptographic verification that stored data is intact and retrievable, without reading the entire dataset.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard 11.11: PoR (Juels-Kaliski 2007, Shacham-Waters 2008) allows a verifier to challenge a storage system with random queries. The storage system responds with proofs that specific blocks exist and are correct. If the proofs verify, the entire dataset is retrievable with high probability.

## DESIGN
- Challenge: verifier sends random block indices + random coefficients
- Response: storage computes linear combination of challenged blocks weighted by coefficients
- Verification: verifier checks response against precomputed authenticators
- Authenticators: computed once at file creation, stored alongside data

## APPLICABILITY
- Future feature: useful for cloud/remote storage verification
- Complementary to RaptorQ: PoR proves data exists, RaptorQ repairs it if it doesn't
- Useful for auditing: periodic PoR challenges prove filesystem integrity without full scan

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] PoR authenticator generation at file creation
- [ ] Challenge-response protocol implemented
- [ ] Verification: false positive rate < 2^-128
- [ ] Benchmark: challenge/response time for 1GB file","acceptance_criteria":"PoR authenticator generation at file creation time. Challenge-response protocol implemented with configurable challenge count. Verification: false positive rate below 2^-128. Authenticators stored alongside data in reserved metadata area. Benchmark: challenge/response time for 1GB file. Benchmark: authenticator generation time per MB. Unit tests: generate authenticators, challenge, verify for small files. Unit tests: corrupted block causes verification failure. Unit tests: uncorrupted file passes all challenges. Property test: random challenge sets always verify on intact data.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:32.840799452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:32.111884373Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crypto","durability","por"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7tn4","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:32.828805088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-7tn4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

+cargo run --example self_heal_demo
+
